column17: My strategy was to evaluate each trade offer carefully based on the value of the chips I had and the chips being offered. I aimed to make trades that would either maintain or slightly increase my chip value. I focused on trading chips that I valued less for those I valued more while also considering the trades being made by others. I tried to avoid losing too much value in the trades while keeping an eye on how others were playing to ensure I didn't fall behind.
column18: I have some experience with strategic decision-making in competitive settings which helped me evaluate the pros and cons of each trade. Additionally I considered that I wanted to make gradual small gains rather than risk large losses. This mindset likely influenced my cautious approach to trades prioritizing chip value over rapid expansion.
column23: While I felt confident about my trades during the game I recognize that some decisions might not have been the best. The dynamic nature of the trading where everyone has different goals and values made it challenging to predict how others would react. That said I did try to maintain a balanced approach to my trades aiming for steady improvements without risking too much at once.
column24: Overall the experience was enjoyable and the gameplay was straightforward once I got into it. However I did find the concept of trade value and how it fluctuated with different chip values a bit tricky at first. A clearer explanation of how the random chip values work or a practice round before the actual game would have been helpful. Additionally it would be great to know if there's any strategy to anticipate what other players might value more or how they might respond as this would add an extra layer to the decision-making process.
